glmassume 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Diagnosing GLMs with glmassume

Plot Zero-Inflation Diagnostic

Source: R/plot_zero_inflation.R
plot_zero_inflation.Rd

Compares the observed proportion of zeros against the expected proportion under the fitted count model. A bar chart shows observed vs expected zero counts side by side, making it easy to detect zero inflation.

Usage

plot_zero_inflation(model, guide = getOption("glmassume.guide", TRUE))

Arguments

model

A fitted glm object with family = poisson or a MASS::glm.nb() model.

guide

Logical. Show interpretive subtitle? Defaults to getOption("glmassume.guide", TRUE).

Value

A ggplot object.

Details

For Poisson models, the expected number of zeros is sum(dpois(0, fitted(model))). For negative binomial models, it is sum(dnbinom(0, mu = fitted(model), size = model$theta)).

If the observed zero count is substantially larger than expected, the data may be zero-inflated and a zero-inflated or hurdle model might be more appropriate.

Examples

counts <- c(18, 17, 15, 20, 10, 20, 25, 13, 12)
outcome <- gl(3, 1, 9)
treatment <- gl(3, 3)
m <- glm(counts ~ outcome + treatment, family = poisson)
plot_zero_inflation(m)
